channel.title: HoopsHype Rumors - Toronto Raptors
channel.description: Toronto Raptors trade rumors and free agency buzz from HoopsHype

item.title: What about New Orleans for Gobert? Toronto. They were …
item.description: Darren Wolfson: "I just I've always come to the conclusion, if Rudy Gobert is playing 30 to 35 minutes a night, you're not winning a championship. I've thought that for years, I continue to think that, but he's an incredible floor raiser. Like if you're Charlotte, he would be great. If you're New Orleans, think about hiring defensive-minded head coach Jamal Mosley. What about New Orleans for Gobert? Toronto. They were good, but Jakob Poeltl, like no. Toronto's had interest in Gobert for a while.

item.title: 2025-26 Kia NBA All-Rookie Second Team:
item.description: NBA Communications: The 2025-26 Kia NBA All-Rookie Second Team: Ace Bailey, @utahjazz Jeremiah Fears, @PelicansNBA Collin Murray-Boyles, @Raptors Derik Queen, @PelicansNBA Maxime Raynaud, @SacramentoKings

item.title: NBA exec says Dawn Staley could be candidate for Mavs head coaching job
item.description: Dawn Staley later said that going through that process made her believe that “no NBA team is ready for a female coach right now.” It’s unclear if Staley would still consider a move to the NBA after that experience. Ujiri interviewed Hammon — the head coach of the WNBA’s Las Vegas Aces — for the Raptors job three years ago. One NBA executive told The Athletic that it wouldn’t be shocking if the Mavericks wanted to interview Staley.

item.title: The 2025-26 Kia NBA All-Defensive Second Team:
item.description: NBA Communications: The 2025-26 Kia NBA All-Defensive Second Team: ▪️ Bam Adebayo, @MiamiHEAT ▪️ OG Anunoby, @nyknicks ▪️ Scottie Barnes, @Raptors ▪️ Dyson Daniels, @ATLHawks ▪️ Cason Wallace, @okcthunder

item.title: Mavericks interested in adding Patrick Englebrecht, Prosper Karangwa to their front office
item.description: According to league sources, the Mavericks have expressed interest in adding Philadelphia’s Prosper Karangwa to their front office, and Toronto’s director of global scouting, Patrick Englebrecht, is also a candidate to reunite with Ujiri in Dallas after they worked together since the 2013-14 season.

item.title: Charlie Villanueva doesn't have Shai Gilgeous-Alexander in his Top 10 point guards ever
item.description: Charlie Villanueva on Shai Gilgeous-Alexander: I wouldn't even put him in the top 10. Q. Give me 10 point guards, combo point guards, it doesn’t matter. Villanueva: Magic Johnson, Isiah Thomas, Stephen Curry, Allen Iverson, John Stockton… I wouldn't put him up past John Stockton, fam. Q. But that's only five you've named so far. Villanueva: Okay, let me get there! Steve Nash, Chris Paul… Okay. I'm cool. I'm cool with all these… Oh, also Tony Parker. (…) He’s not in front of Jason Kidd. (…) Bob C

item.title: “Of course I wanted to play,” Anunoby told The …
item.description: OG Anunoby is an NBA champion. That will never be taken away from him, even if the history books don’t tell the whole story. As a second-year forward with the Toronto Raptors, the up-and-coming Anunoby played a big role primarily off the bench for Canada’s team as it earned 58 wins and the No. 2 seed during the regular season. Right before the playoffs, however, Anunoby underwent an emergency appendectomy, forcing him to miss the entire playoff run that culminated in the Raptors winning their fi

item.title: "I was younger back then, I didn't realize how hard it …
item.description: SNY Knicks: "I was younger back then, I didn't realize how hard it [was] to get back...getting back is amazing, takes a lot - I'm really excited" OG Anunoby, who made the NBA Finals as a member of the Raptors in 2019 but did not play, on being there with the Knicks:

item.title: NBA will have a different NBA champion for 8th …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: The NBA will have a different NBA champion for 8th straight season, extending the longest streak in league history. 2019 - Toronto 2020 - Los Angeles Lakers 2021 - Milwaukee 2022 - Golden State 2023 - Denver 2024 - Boston 2025 - Oklahoma City 2026 - New York/San Antonio

item.title: RJ Barrett is in attendance for today’s press …
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: Rowan Barrett, GM of the senior men’s team, and the program’s new head coach Gordie Herbert are announcing Canada Basketball’s player pool for this summer’s FIBA World Cup qualifiers. RJ Barrett is in attendance for today’s press conference.

item.title: NBAers in for Canada this summer:
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: To sum up NBAers in for Canada this summer: SGA, NAW, Barrett, Brooks, Dort, Nembhard bros, Mathurin, Olynyk, George, Miller Out this summer but in for this cycle: Edey, Powell Didn’t commit: Murray, Sharpe, Wiggins Committed but not selected: Lawson, Banton, Houstan, Prosper

item.title: Chris Paul on Victor Wembanyama: Wemby is going to make you question everything you've ever learned about the game
item.description: NBA on ESPN: "[Victor Wembanyama is going] to make you question everything you've even learned about the game." -- Chris Paul shares his thoughts on Wemby's impact on the court

item.title: Tre Donaldson has completed NBA pre-draft workouts …
item.description: Tobias Bass: Miami’s Tre Donaldson has completed NBA pre-draft workouts with the Jazz, Suns, Spurs, Lakers, Warriors, Clippers, Knicks He will also workout with the Hornets, Bucks, Heat, Kings, Raptors, Celtics, Pistons, Magic, Nuggets and Trail Blazers

item.title: Clippers unlikely to trade Kawhi Leonard?
item.description: As we’ve seen previously with Leonard and the Toronto Raptors, even winning a title isn’t enough to keep him around long term. What’s more, as one rival executive from another team that has an interest in Leonard shared with The Athletic, there is skepticism that Clippers owner Steve Ballmer is truly willing to trade Leonard. Since Pistons president of basketball operations Trajan Langdon has been at the helm, he’s prioritized optionality. Neither Duren, 22, nor Thompson, 23, has reached their p

item.title: “The first time [in the NBA Finals] was really cool, …
item.description: OG Anunoby — who was sidelined by injury during the Raptors’ 2019 title run — didn’t make much more of an impact for most of Game 1 of the NBA Finals, but the soft-spoken wing made sure he was heard down the stretch Wednesday night, scoring 12 of his 17 points in the fourth quarter of the Knicks’ 105-95 win over the Spurs at Frost Bank Center. “The first time [in the NBA Finals] was really cool, but it wasn’t the way I would have wanted it.” Anunoby said. “So this time, just happy to be here, h

item.title: Those include Minnesota's Micah Nori, Houston's Royal …
item.description: Yet the more realistic outcome for Dallas remains hiring someone from within the NBA and, to that end, sources say that the Mavericks have either already registered official interest or plan to do so with a number of prominent assistant coaches. Those include Minnesota's Micah Nori, Houston's Royal Ivey, Toronto's Jama Mahlalela, Boston's Tony Dobbins and Miami consultant Noah LaRoche.

item.title: “If guys don’t commit this summer, they’re not in,” …
item.description: By all accounts, incoming head coach Gordie Herbert – the team’s third bench boss in the span of three years – will continue to enforce it and prioritize continuity. It served the British Columbia native well during his stint at the helm of the German National Team, leading them to a gold medal at the 2023 World Cup and a fourth-place finish in Paris. “If guys don’t commit this summer, they’re not in,” said Herbert, who served as an assistant with the Raptors in 2008-09 and has coached around th

item.title: At the NBA House pop-up event in Montreal promoting …
item.description: At the NBA House pop-up event in Montreal promoting the league final between the New York Knicks and San Antonio Spurs, a former champion expressed enthusiasm about the city one day hosting a franchise in basketball’s highest league. Serge Ibaka, a 2019 champion with the Toronto Raptors, Canada’s only NBA franchise, said at La Nesra in Griffintown on Friday that the second largest market in the country “deserves a team.” “The way things are here, it’s a beautiful city. I feel people love sport i

item.title: Bobby Webster, via press release: "We're building …
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: Bobby Webster, via press release: "We're building something special here and I'm proud to continue to lead the Toronto Raptors as we work towards our next championship... This is a franchise that's focused on the future and we're ready for what's next."

item.title: MLSE CEO Keith Pelley: "Bobby (Webster) has an elite …
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: MLSE CEO Keith Pelley: "Bobby (Webster) has an elite understanding of the game and the NBA and he's used that to build a team that competes at a high level... With Bobby and the Raptors, we've seen the results and we believe in where his leadership will take us."

item.title: Raptors sign GM Bobby Webster to a multi-year contract extension
item.description: Blake Murphy: The Raptors have extended Bobby Webster on a multi-year deal, and extended a handful of other front office members. Webster now also has an EVP title.

item.title: Other extensions: Dan Tolzman, vice-president, player …
item.description: Doug Smith: Other extensions: Dan Tolzman, vice-president, player personnel and assistant general manager; Keith Boyarsky, vice-president, basketball strategy and research; and Tyla Flexman, vice-president, operations. Next up: New deal for coach Darko Rajakovic expected any time soon

item.title: Raptors to extend Darko Rajakovic?
item.description: The next obvious move is an extension for Raptors head coach Darko Rajakovic, who is also entering the final year of his existing contract. The timing on that move has yet to be determined, but multiple sources have confirmed it’s in the works.

item.title: JR Smith: Michael Jordan is my GOAT
item.description: JR Smith: I did not grow up a Knicks fan. I hated the Knicks. I did not like Patrick Ewing. I did not like John Starks. I didn't like anything about the Knicks. I was a Bulls fan. I was a Michael Jordan fan. Michael Jordan is my GOAT. That's it. And my agent calls me and he gives he gives me these list of teams. Only thing I kept bringing me back was playing with Chris Paul. He's going to pass the ball. I'm going to be in a great situation. They play fast. They play up and down. My type of style

item.title: Sources say Charlotte, though, is not currently …
item.description: There have been multiple reports this week about Sacramento and Charlotte discussing Domantas Sabonis trade frameworks, but sources say those conversations were preliminary in nature and reflective of the Kings' desire to acquire the Hornets' No. 14 or No. 18 pick. Sources say Charlotte, though, is not currently willing to surrender either pick. I'm told that the Kings also have interest in Toronto's No. 19 selection, but it is unclear whether the Raptors, who showed trade interest in Sabonis b

item.title: The Raptors continue to hold pre-draft workouts. Among …
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: The Raptors continue to hold pre-draft workouts. Among the prospects they've hosted so far, per sources: Meleek Thomas, Bennett Stirtz, Zuby Ejiofor, Keyshawn Hall, Izaiyah Nelson, Peter Suder, Tyler Bilodeau, Michael Ajayi, Bruce Thornton. They own picks # 19 & 50 on June 23/24.

item.title: "My Raptors teammates that I used to play with in the …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: OG on who has reached out to him on his tip in GW: "My Raptors teammates that I used to play with in the past, they hit me up. They were really excited for me, happy for me"

item.title: Mavericks expected to turn to rising NBA assistant for head coaching job
item.description: In the wake of Dallas moving on from Jason Kidd, there have been rumblings of both Duke's Jon Scheyer and Michigan's Dusty May being linked to the vacancy. But ever since Masai Ujiri was hired to run basketball operations last month, the belief around the league is that the most likely outcome for Dallas is elevating one of the NBA's rising assistants. That's what Ujiri did the other two times he hired a coach, both with the Toronto Raptors: In 2018, he replaced Dwane Casey with Nick Nurse. In 2

item.title: And it joins only Toronto in 2018-19, according to the …
item.description: This team Leon Rose built is the NBA's first to win the Larry O’Brien Trophy and NBA Cup double in the same season. It just beat the Spurs four times in five tries despite spotting a double-digit lead to San Antonio in the first quarter of all five games. And it joins only Toronto in 2018-19, according to the Elias Sports Bureau, as NBA champions with four starters acquired via trade. These Knicks and those Raptors are also the league's only two teams, per Elias, to open a Finals-clinching game

item.title: And Nelson, I'm told, was quietly interviewed by the …
item.description: That's the same title Donnie Nelson held with the Mavericks for nearly 20 years. And Nelson, I'm told, was quietly interviewed by the Raptors last summer after Ujiri's departure as part of a search they conducted in the buildup to announcing Webster as Ujiri’s full-time successor in August 2025. Former Raptors coach Dwane Casey, who currently holds an advisory role with Detroit, also featured in Toronto's search.

item.title: Hawaii native Bobby Webster was quietly pursued by the …
item.description: A little over a year ago, league sources say, Hawaii native Bobby Webster was quietly pursued by the University of Hawaii when the Rainbow Warriors needed a new athletic director. Webster, at the time, was still Toronto's No. 2 executive under Masai Ujiri. But there was no luring him away from the NBA or the Raptors — not even by the big school where he grew up — and that reality soon made perfect sense. One day after last June's draft, Toronto abruptly parted ways with Ujiri and would install W

item.title: Dan Tolzman says Raptors like being at 19. Feel …
item.description: Ryan Woltstat: Dan Tolzman says Raptors like being at 19. Feel somebody can fall and it isn't crucial you workout or interview a prospect before taking him. Points to Ja'Kobe Walter who didn't do either with them. They believed in the intel they gathered on him.

item.title: - Draft considered wide open after top 10-12 - Keeping …
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: Notes from asst Raptors GM Dan Tolzman’s pre-draft avail: - Have done most of their workouts, digging in on prospects they like - No major differences in process under Bobby Webster - Draft considered wide open after top 10-12 - Keeping options open but cost to move up is high, happy at 19

item.title: Bennett Stirtz tells me he will be attending the NBA …
item.description: Elliot Clough: NEW: Bennett Stirtz tells me he will be attending the NBA Draft at the Barclays Center in Brooklyn, New York next Tuesday. Coach Ben McCollum will join Stirtz and his family in the green room. So far, Stirtz has worked out with the following teams: Charlotte Hornets, Chicago Bulls, Dallas Mavericks, Memphis Grizzlies, Oklahoma City Thunder, Toronto Raptors. He'll also workout for the Golden State Warriors prior to the draft.

item.title: Stirtz has worked out with the following teams:
item.description: Elliot Clough: Bennett Stirtz tells me he will be attending the NBA Draft at the Barclays Center in Brooklyn, New York next Tuesday. Coach Ben McCollum will join Stirtz and his family in the green room. So far, Stirtz has worked out with the following teams: Charlotte Hornets Chicago Bulls Dallas Mavericks Memphis Grizzlies Oklahoma City Thunder Toronto Raptors He'll also workout for the Golden State Warriors prior to the draft.

item.title: Hornets have Nic Claxton on their radar
item.description: With Charlotte considering a starting-caliber center upgrade, other centers besides Sabonis are on their radar, including Brooklyn Nets center Nic Claxton, league sources told HoopsHype. In addition, it’s worth noting that the Nets have expressed interest in Hornets free agent guard Coby White in the past when he was on the Chicago Bulls, sources said. In other Hornets news, assistant coach Zach Peterson is a candidate to become the franchise’s next head coach of their G League affiliate, the Gr

item.title: On the Mavericks' HC search: There is a persistent …
item.description: Marc Stein: On the Mavericks' HC search: There is a persistent belief in coaching circles that Toronto's Jama Mahlalela, along with Minnesota's Micah Nori and Houston's Royal Ivey as covered yesterday, continues to factor into Masai Ujiri's thinking if he opts to a hire a current assistant

item.title: Zohran Mamdani shouts out a few former Knicks for …
item.description: Fred Katz: Zohran Mamdani shouts out a few former Knicks for laying the foundation a championship, including Immanuel Quickley, RJ Barrett, Donte DiVincenzo, Julius Randle and Tom Thibodeau.

item.title: RJ Barrett drawing trade interest
item.description: Toronto Raptors swingman RJ Barrett has drawn trade interest in multi-team conversations, league sources told HoopsHype. The Canadian swingman is on an expiring $29.62 million contract this upcoming season. He’s averaged 20.5 points on 49.5 percent shooting from the field and 35.3 percent beyond the arc with 5.9 rebounds and 4.3 assists since joining his hometown Raptors.

item.title: Raptors could be interested in Myles Turner?
item.description: A couple of league sources have suggested that the Raptors could be interested in Bucks centre Myles Turner if the Bucks go into fire-sale mode after a potential Antetokounmpo trade.

item.title: It’s expected the Toronto Raptors will announce a …
item.description: It’s expected the Toronto Raptors will announce a multi-year contract extension for head coach Darko Rajakovic early this week, both as a reward for a job well done and a bet that he can continue to develop as a longtime leader of a team that’s trying to transition from "pretty good" to "actually good" and, ideally, much better than that over the life of the deal.

item.title: Per sources, it’s a move that Dick would be open to as …
item.description: The most likely development as the off-season unfolds will be a new address for Gradey Dick, the fourth-year wing who was leapfrogged in the rotation by Ja'Kobe Walter, Jamison Battle and A.J. Lawson in the final weeks of the season. Per sources, it’s a move that Dick would be open to as he looks for a fresh start and a chance to pick up from his promising second season (14.4 points per game with 54 starts) after things went south in Year 3 (six points per game with one start).

item.title: Yaxel Lendeborg is the oldest Top-11 pick in 22 years
item.description: Jorge Sierra: Yaxel Lendeborg is the oldest Top 11 pick in the draft since Toronto selected Rafael Araujo back in 2004. Just an FYI.

item.title: Allen Graves heads to Toronto at No. 19
item.description: Dan Woike: St. Mary's Allen Graves is the guy here for TOR, I'm hearing.

item.title: Toronto Raptors will select Allen Graves with the No. …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Toronto Raptors will select Allen Graves with the No. 19 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft.

item.title: That's always been our philosophy ... Sometimes you …
item.description: William Lou: “It's always been best-available, two-way players. That's always been our philosophy ... Sometimes you get into a trap if you try to take for fit in the first round." Bobby Webster on the Raptors drafting so many big two-way forwards

item.title: Myles Turner an option for Raptors?
item.description: NBA sources I’ve spoken with have connected the Raptors to Bucks centre Myles Turner, with the possibility that Raptors centre Jakob Poeltl, third-year wing Gradey Dick and potentially draft compensation being the core of a deal that would net Toronto the veteran big with credentials as a rim protector and three-point threat.

item.title: Raptors go with Jaden Bradley at No. 50
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Toronto Raptors will select Jaden Bradley with the 50th pick of the 2026 NBA Draft.

item.title: Raptors will select Jaden Bradley at No. 50,
item.description: Jake Fischer: The Raptors will select Jaden Bradley at No. 50, sources say.

item.title: Wolves, Raptors emerge as teams interested in LaMelo Ball
item.description: Marc Stein: The Timberwolves and Raptors have emerged as two of the teams with trade interest in Charlotte’s LaMelo Ball, league sources tell @JakeLFischer and me. More to come …

item.title: LaMelo Ball has drawn trade interest from several …
item.description: ‪Michael Scotto‬: LaMelo Ball has drawn trade interest from several teams, including the Toronto Raptors and Minnesota Timberwolves, sources confirm. Toronto originally expressed trade interest in Ball during the summer of 2025 and renewed talks with the Charlotte Hornets over the past few days.

item.title: "Maybe his former teams." One of the most fascinating …
item.description: Kevin O'Connor: "Maybe his former teams." One of the most fascinating comments of the night by @BobbyMarks42 regarding which teams could be interested in trading for Kawhi Leonard. Now, I can't imagine it's the Spurs. But the Raptors? Yeah. Sign me up for Ingram + whatever it takes for Kawhi.

item.title: Nimari Burnett has agreed to a deal with the Toronto …
item.description: Jake Weingarten: Michigan’s Nimari Burnett has agreed to a deal with the Toronto Raptors, source told @Stockrisers . Started all 40 games this season for the national champions.

item.title: "I knew a few off the top of my head, but I hadn't …
item.description: He will become the 13th player in NBA history to reach a 20th season, joining LeBron James, Vince Carter, Chris Paul, Dirk Nowitzki, Kevin Garnett, Kevin Willis, Robert Parish, Kyle Lowry, Udonis Haslem, Jamal Crawford, Kobe Bryant and Kareem Abdul-Jabbar. "I knew a few off the top of my head, but I hadn't seen the list," Al Horford told ESPN. "It's hard to believe. It's something that, for me, I'm very grateful. It's just so hard to be in this league for that amount of years. There's a lot of c

item.title: Kawhi Leonard would be willing to sign extensions with Raptors, Spurs
item.description: While the LA Clippers are undeniably determined to keep hold of Kawhi Leonard — and with Leonard's clear first choice known to be staying with them in his beloved Southern California — league sources say that Toronto has registered interest in trying to re-acquire the engine of the lone championship team in franchise history should Leonard become truly available.

item.title: Sources say he has a similar view regarding a …
item.description: And sources say that Leonard would not have interest in signing an extension with Detroit in the event that the Pistons traded for him this summer. Sources say he has a similar view regarding a long-term partnership with the Heat if Miami had any designs on pursuing Leonard to slot alongside Antetokounmpo and Bam Adebayo.

item.title: Toronto and San Antonio
item.description: Sources close to the situation tell The Stein Line that, when it comes to potential trade talks, there are only two other teams that Leonard would consider signing an extension with: The two teams he played for before becoming a Clipper. Toronto and San Antonio.

item.title: Raptors exercise 2026-27 team option on Jamal Shead
item.description: Michael Scotto: JUST IN: The Toronto Raptors have exercised the $2.30 million team option on Jamal Shead for the 2026-27 season, league sources told @hoopshype . Shead played all 82 games, averaging 6.6 points and 5.4 assist per game for Toronto last season.

item.title: Two-way player Chucky Hepburn to sign qualifying offer with Raptors
item.description: Michael Scotto: Toronto Raptors guard Chucky Hepburn will sign his two-way qualifying offer and return to the team next season, league sources told @hoopshype. He averaged 12.4 points and shot 36.8% from 3-point range with 8.6 assists and 2.4 steals for game with the Raptors 905 in the G League.

item.title: Chucky Hepburn has made good progress in his recovery …
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: Chucky Hepburn has made good progress in his recovery from season-ending knee surgery & is somebody the Raptors are excited to have back. Hepburn had a strong camp/preseason last year & would've seen some run when Immanuel Quickley got hurt late in the season. Another tough, defensive-minded PG that can play

item.title: "I've heard Sandro Mamukelashvili from Toronto as …
item.description: "I've heard Sandro Mamukelashvili from Toronto as potentially an option there (for Minnesota). Now, I think that's going to depend on what his free agent market place ends up being."

item.title: Timberwolves explored James Harden, Ja Morant as point guard options
item.description: At the same time, teams like Minnesota and Toronto — franchises that had been extremely aggressive in pursuing upgrades — found themselves with an opportunity to improve their fortunes. The Wolves, after sending Julius Randle to Brooklyn, were left with a $33 million trade exception and a cap book $66 million leaner, while continuing to signal intentions to add a quality point guard, sources say (a pursuit that at one time included Cleveland's James Harden and Memphis' Ja Morant). The Raptors, w

item.title: The Raptors were another rumored suitor for the point …
item.description: Ball, 24, is under contract for the next three seasons and is set to make $40.8 million next year as a running mate for Anthony Edwards in Minnesota. The Raptors were another rumored suitor for the point guard and league sources confirmed to MassLive that the Celtics did not pursue a deal for the former Hornets guard.

item.title: Kawhi Leonard would not sign extension if traded to Pistons
item.description: The Kawhi Leonard story featured above revealed Toronto's interest in exploring trades to try to reacquire the best player they've ever had to team with Scottie Barnes … with sources saying that Leonard would indeed have the Raptors on his short list of teams that he's open to signing a contract extension with in the event that his seven-season relationship with the Clippers came to an end. One of the most notable details from that reporting: Leonard would not be amenable to extending his contra

item.title: A couple league sources confirmed that the Raptors …
item.description: That doesn’t mean that they weren’t or won’t be in the mix for other players. A couple league sources confirmed that the Raptors were interested in getting involved in talks with the Charlotte Hornets for mercurial point guard LaMelo Ball, a player Webster has always liked, which is one of the reasons he was intrigued by the Hornets' top job before current Charlotte GM Jeff Peterson was hired in 2024.

item.title: One league source I spoke with said that the Clippers …
item.description: There are mixed signals around the NBA on the subject of his availability and the possibility of him being moved, with reports circulating that the two-time Finals MVP would be open to reuniting with his former teams, San Antonio and Toronto, though the Houston Rockets could also be part of that mix, per one source. One league source I spoke with said that the Clippers are absolutely not moving him, preferring to let the last year of his contract play out to preserve future flexibility, but stil

item.title: “I think he's getting multiple years guaranteed at …
item.description: Internally, the Raptors have budgeted a deal in the $10-million-per-year range, a number that they can get to if they find a taker for the last year of Dick’s contract. That would be a considerable raise for Mamukelashvili, who is expected to decline the $2.8-million player option on his contract by Monday’s deadline. The problem is that bigs with his profile are highly valued, with multiple league sources expecting Mamukelashvili to have a robust market in free agency. “I think he's getting mul

item.title: Jaylen Brown trade suitors: Blazers, Raptors, Nuggets, Nets, Hornets, Hawks, Clippers
item.description: Brown has drawn trade interest from various teams across the league, including the Portland Trail Blazers, Toronto Raptors, Denver Nuggets, Brooklyn Nets, Charlotte Hornets, Atlanta Hawks, and Los Angeles Clippers, league sources told HoopsHype.

item.title: Jay Triano, who has coached both the Raptors and the …
item.description: Sources say Jay Triano, who has coached both the Raptors and the Suns in addition to numerous stops as an assistant and a lengthy stint as head coach of Canada's national team, is a leading candidate to join Micah Nori’s first coaching staff in Portland after working on Jason Kidd’s staff in Dallas last season. Nori’s first coaching job in the NBA came after working as an advance scout for more than a decade when Triano made him part of his Raptors coaching staff.

item.title: Kawhi Leonard back to Toronto?
item.description: Jake Fischer: The Raptors and Clippers have held real trade conversations this weekend on sending Kawhi Leonard back to Toronto, according to the league sources.

item.title: Toronto has expressed a preference in dealing Brandon …
item.description: Jake Fischer: In discussions with the Clippers, sources say Toronto has expressed a preference in dealing Brandon Ingram to Los Angeles, as opposed to RJ Barrett.

item.title: League sources say that interest on the part of the …
item.description: League sources say that interest on the part of the Mavericks and Raptors is genuine, though. It’s just a matter of what Clippers owner Steve Ballmer wants to do.

item.title: As far as I can tell, it's still unclear whether …
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: As far as I can tell, it's still unclear whether Clippers are really open to moving Kawhi & whether Kawhi is really open to a Toronto reunion or if they're just posturing. Those are obviously key factors, in addition to what other teams are offering (tho Kawhi market is limited)

item.title: Should also note, as I have before: in 13 years under …
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: Should also note, as I have before: in 13 years under this Masai Ujiri and now Bobby Webster-led front office, the Raptors have never moved multiple 1st-round picks in a trade. Don't expect them to do it for a 35-year-old Kawhi Leonard.

item.title: The Raptors' interest in Kawhi was described to me as …
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: The Raptors' interest in Kawhi was described to me as "real but conditional." Contingent on price, obviously. Hard to see them paying more than last time, so think similar framework: secondary star (Ingram), former lottery pick (Dick), 1st-rounder in the following year's draft.

item.title: The Clippers naturally trying to extract more than has …
item.description: Dialogue between the teams continued Sunday, league sources say, with the Clippers naturally trying to extract more than has been offered if they indeed are parting with their Face of the Franchise, while the Raptors remain adamant about trying to build a deal — which also would naturally include a level of draft capital — around scoring forward Brandon Ingram and Gradey Dick rather than Canadian-born swingman RJ Barrett.

item.title: The Raptors have discussed the possibility of a trade …
item.description: There are plenty of moving parts and some contradictory information kicking around, but two sources close to the situation that I spoke with on Sunday confirmed that the Raptors have discussed the possibility of a trade with the Los Angeles Clippers that would bring the 2026 second-team all-NBA selection back to Toronto. The Raptors' interest in Leonard was first reported by Marc Stein and Jake Fischer.

item.title: The Clippers, furthermore, would have interest in …
item.description: Sources say that Leonard's willingness to sign an extension with the Raptors were he to be traded back to Toronto, as The Stein Line also reported Thursday, has opened the door for a trade to progress to completion. If the Clippers, that is, can ultimately bear to move on from The Kawhi Era. It's believed that the Clippers, furthermore, would have interest in Toronto's offer because adding Ingram — who earned All-Star status last season — should be able to help keep LA sufficiently competitive i

item.title: The most likely construction of the deal would centre …
item.description: The most likely construction of the deal would centre on a package including 2026 all-star Brandon Ingram and third-year wing Gradey Dick, which would work in terms of salary matching, per one source. The Raptors would likely have to add some draft picks as additional compensation.

item.title: Said another: “The Clippers are willing to move him … …
item.description: Said another: “The Clippers are willing to move him … and I think a lot of teams are wary of acquiring Kawhi because you just don't know if he's going to play … there’s risk there, obviously. Toronto had him, and so maybe feels more comfortable that he would play for them, could play for them.”

item.title: Sources are telling me that he’s expected to attract …
item.description: The chatter is building momentum as the NBA heads into the opening of its free agency period on Tuesday night. The Raptors have some business to focus on there, too, with power forward Sandro Mamukelashvili expected to opt out of the final year of his $2.8-million deal on Monday. Sources are telling me that he’s expected to attract multiple offers starting at more than $10 million a year, which could make it challenging for the Raptors to keep him.

item.title: Raptors, Clippers seriously engaged in Kawhi Leonard trade talks
item.description: The LA Clippers and Toronto Raptors are seriously engaged in trade talks for All-NBA forward Kawhi Leonard, sources told ESPN. LA and Toronto discussed a deal over the past couple of weeks, as Leonard's reps informed other teams he only wanted to sign an extension with the Raptors if the Clippers were not going to keep him, sources said. He had preferred to stay with the Clippers, but the franchise made no long-term commitment to him this offseason, sources said. Leonard has only the 2026-27 sea

item.title: If traded, Kawhi Leonard would only sign extension with Raptors
item.description: NBA Courtside: JUST IN: The Toronto Raptors are the only team Kawhi Leonard will extend with if he’s traded from the Clippers, per @ShamsCharania

item.title: Lou Williams on rumors of the Clippers trading Kawhi …
item.description: Run It Back: Lou Williams on rumors of the Clippers trading Kawhi to the Raptors: "I didn't see this on my bingo card, but it makes sense to me." 👀 @MichelleDBeadle | @ChandlerParsons | @TeamLou23

item.title: We know Atlanta isn’t going after Goga Bitadze, Hawks …
item.description: Jake Fischer: Two trades that we’re monitoring today… The Clippers and Raptors’continuing conversations on Kawhi Leonard, plus the Hawks working on a big addition. We know Atlanta isn’t going after Goga Bitadze, Hawks fans, as the Magic aren’t talking trade for their reserve center, sources say, while anticipating Mo Wagner will leave Orlando in free agency.

item.title: Sandro Mamukelashvili declining player option to hit free agency
item.description: Shams Charania: Toronto Raptors big man Sandro Mamukelashvili is declining his $2.8 million player option for 2026-27 to become a free agent, George Roussakis and Mark Bartelstein of @PrioritySports tell ESPN. Mamukelashvili will have a strong developing market in free agency.

item.title: Raptors want Mamu back and he wants to come back but, …
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: Raptors want Mamu back and he wants to come back but, with their tax crunch & (cough) aspirations in the trade market, they’ll need to dump salary to make a competitive offer. He may have priced himself out of a return with his strong season (similar to Biyombo situation in 2016)

item.title: Raptors exercising player option on Trayce Jackson-Davis
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Toronto Raptors are exercising the $2.41 million team option on Trayce Jackson-Davis, league sources told @hoopshype . The 26-year-old forward has averaged 6.2 points on 63.1% shooting from the field and 4.4 rebounds in 14.2 minutes in 3 seasons with the Warriors and Raptors.

item.title: The Toronto Raptors are opting into Trayce …
item.description: Shams Charania: The Toronto Raptors are opting into Trayce Jackson-Davis's $2.4 million team option for the 2026-27 season, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: Plenty of noise around Kawhi Leonard/Clippers/Raptors …
item.description: Michael Grange: Plenty of noise around Kawhi Leonard/Clippers/Raptors *potential* deal, but my understanding is second-year wing Ja’Kobe Walter would NOT be part of any deal the Raptors might make, per sources. It almost goes without saying prize rookie Colin Murray-Boyles is off limits also:

item.title: 2026 First-round pick Bennett Stirtz: 'I thought I was going to be Division II my whole career'
item.description: During the pre-NBA Draft process, Bennett Stirtz worked out with the Chicago Bulls, Oklahoma City Thunder, Golden State Warriors, Memphis Grizzlies, Toronto Raptors and others. Now he’s in New York, getting ready for his name to be called at the 2026 NBA Draft. A few years ago, not even Stirtz thought this was possible. “Not at all,” Stirtz said. “I thought I was going to be Division II my whole career.”

item.title: Shelburne: Kawhi Leonard has been very comfortable in Toronto
item.description: Ramona Shelburne: "From what I understand, Kawhi is comfortable going back to places he's been comfortable before. He's been very comfortable in Toronto. There's a guy who runs their performance there and he managed him the entire season with all the load managing and all the injuries he had, all the way to a championship. He's very comfortable with the Raptors organization and he didn't burn any bridges. He never promised them anything. He always was clear about where he was thinking about goin

item.title: That deal works as far as from a financial standpoint, …
item.description: Bobby Marks: "If he is traded, he is eligible to sign a two-year, $124 million extension with the Toronto Raptors. It would have to be Brandon Ingram, who has two years left, there is a player option here, and then to make the math work, it would be Gradey Dick. Now here is where we are kind of in a little bit of a snag, draft picks. Toronto has four first-round picks to trade, and if you are the Clippers, you would certainly probably want 2027, 2029, and 2031. That is awfully rich. If I am Toro

item.title: Shams: Kawhi Leonard's time with the Clippers is nearing its end
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Shams on Kawhi: "Those talks are continuing, they're serious between the Clippers and the Raptors...all signs point to these two sides being motivated and the future is nearing it's end Kawhi Leonard's tenure is nearing it's end in Los Angeles. He has not been given any indication that the Clippers want to give him a contract extension"

item.title: Toronto Raptors are willing to offer Kawhi Leonard the …
item.description: APHoops: The Toronto Raptors are willing to offer Kawhi Leonard the 2-year $126.1 million max contract extension he is demanding, per @ShamsCharania . 2026-27: $50.3M 2027-28: $61.5M 2028-29: $64.5M Total: $176.4M

item.title: Clippers not involved in Jaylen Brown trade talks?
item.description: Also important: Sources say that the Clippers, whether or not their talks with the Raptors progress all the way to a completed deal, have not pursued Boston's Jaylen Brown this offseason. The sources added that the Clippers aren't planning to make a run at the Celtics' MVP candidate.

item.title: "Those talks are continuing, they're serious between …
item.description: Shams on Kawhi: "Those talks are continuing, they're serious between the Clippers and the Raptors...all signs point to these two sides being motivated and the future is nearing it's end Kawhi Leonard's tenure is nearing it's end in Los Angeles. He has not been given any indication that the Clippers want to give him a contract extension"

item.title: Ja’Kobe Walter would not be part of any deal the Raptors might make for Kawhi Leonard
item.description: Michael Grange: Plenty of noise around Leonard/Clippers/Raptors *potential* deal, but my understanding is second-year wing Ja’Kobe Walter would NOT be part of any deal the Raptors might make, per sources. It almost goes without saying prize rookie Colin Murray-Boyles is off limits also:

item.title: Lakers eyeing Sandro Mamukelashvili
item.description: But people around the NBA, tasked with trying to make sense of the evolving free-agency landscape, started to link the Lakers to a wide range of players throughout Monday. One name has come up from multiple league sources: Toronto forward/center Sandro Mamukelashvili. Mamukelashvili declined his $2.8 million option with the Raptors Monday, with league sources believing he could command $10 million or more in free agency.

item.title: Kawhi Leonard trade 'could happen today' as Clippers, Raptors continue discussing draft pick compensation
item.description: Brian Windhorst on potential Kawhi Leonard trade to Raptors: This is lining up. This trade could happen today. I think the Clippers and the Raptors have agreed on player compensation. I think it would be Brandon Ingram based on what I am told that would head to Los Angeles. What they've been haggling over is the draft pick compensation. The Raptors have all of their four first-round picks. They have up to four to trade. Naturally, the Clippers are going to want multiple of those. Let's see if th

item.title: Clippers, Raptors spent Monday nearing agreement on a Kawhi Leonard trade
item.description: Jake Fischer: The Clippers and Raptors spent Monday nearing a framework that would send Kawhi Leonard back to Toronto, sources say. Will those negotiations be completed before free agency officially opens at 6 p.m. ET?

item.title: Clippers, Raptors finalizing deal to send Kawhi Leonard back to Toronto
item.description: Shams Charania: BREAKING: The Los Angeles Clippers are nearing deal sending Kawhi Leonard to the Toronto Raptors for Brandon Ingram, Gradey Dick, 2 first-round picks, 1 pick swap and 2 second-rounders, sources tell ESPN. A return to Canada for the Raptors champion and two-time Finals MVP.

item.title: Lakers interested in Quentin Grimes
item.description: Marc Stein: Last night we reported the Lakers' rising interest in Toronto free agent Sandro Mamukelashvili. Now league sources tell @JakeLFischer and me that Philadelphia's Quentin Grimes is emerging as another top Lakers target. MORE Intel here: tinyurl.com/bde3f33k

item.title: Brandon Ingram and Gradey Dick plus picks for Kawhi …
item.description: Michael Grange: Not saying this is not going to happen, but per league sources, Brandon Ingram and Gradey Dick plus picks for Kawhi Leonard is not yet completed. Some details still TBD.

item.title: The Raptors luxury tax/apron situation is fluid due to …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: The Raptors luxury tax/apron situation is fluid due to Kawhi Leonard's $7.45 million trade bonus. He will need to waive most of it unless Raptors shed more payroll. They'd have enough room to fill their final four roster spots with minimum salaries if he waives it all.

item.title: Leonard has the 2026-27 season left on his contract at …
item.description: Leonard has the 2026-27 season left on his contract at $50.3 million. He had preferred to stay with the Clippers, but the franchise made no long-term commitments to him this offseason, sources said.

item.title: Familiarity with a Toronto organization that has the …
item.description: Three key reasons Leonard was open to this deal to Toronto, according to sources: Familiarity with a Toronto organization that has the majority of its front office structure in place, now led by executive vice president Bobby Webster. Leonard also likes the city of Toronto and believes the Raptors can contend in the Eastern Conference.

item.title: Because of where Toronto is with regard to the first …
item.description: Bobby Marks: Because of where Toronto is with regard to the first apron, the Kawhi Leonard proposed trade will need to wait until July 6. Leonard also has a 15% trade bonus of his remaining salary. The bonus is valued at $7.5M and the Clippers are responsible to pay. He can amend the bonus down or eliminate.

item.title: Kawhi Leonard will work with Raptors to sign two-year extension
item.description: Chris Haynes: Kawhi Leonard and Toronto Raptors will soon engage to structure out the terms on a two-year extension to lock up the future Hall of Famer through the 2028-29 season, sources say.

item.title: Leonard's and Ingram's could take a minute
item.description: Michael Grange: Given the histories of the primary players involved, it's not surprising that the last hurdle to get a deal done is each team signing off on the medicals of the incoming player, per league source. Gradey Dick? Shouldn't be a problem. Leonard's and Ingram's could take a minute.

item.title: High scoring average in a single postseason in Raptors …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: High scoring average in a single postseason in Raptors history: Kawhi Leonard - 30.5 in 2019 Vince Carter - 27.3 in 2001 RJ Barrett - 24.1 this year Scottie Barnes - 24.1 this year Chris Bosh - 24.0 in 2008

item.title: Kawhi Leonard to Raptors trade has been completed
item.description: Shams Charania Tweets‬: Kawhi Leonard to the Raptors has been agreed to, sources tell ESPN. The Clippers will receive unprotected first-round picks in 2031 and 2033, 2030 and 2033 second-round picks and a 2027 first-round swap.

item.title: They'll have to maximize these next few years with …
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: That changes the risk level for the Raptors to HIGH. Leonard will be 40 and 42 in 2031 and 2033. So, likely retired. They'll have to maximize these next few years with Kawhi and figure out a way to remain competitive post-Kawhi or those picks could be juicy. Raps are going for it

item.title: Clippers get the two most valuable first-round picks …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: The Clippers get the two most valuable first-round picks the Raptors could offer and should move up around 10 spots in the 2027 Draft with that swap. Fantastic return for Kawhi Leonard.

item.title: Raptors sending unprotected first round picks in 2031 …
item.description: ‪Doug Smith‬: NBA sources: Raptors sending unprotected first round picks in 2031 and 2033, second round picks in 2030 and 2033 and the right to swap picks in 2027 to the Clips to finalize Leonard deal

item.title: Jamison Battle's contract with the Toronto Raptors …
item.description: ‪KeithSmithNBA‬: Jamison Battle's contract with the Toronto Raptors becomes fully guaranteed for $2.3M if he's not waived today.

item.title: The Raptors turned away requests for all-NBA rookie …
item.description: The deal came together over the past 48 hours. Per sources, the two sides engaged in elevated discussions over the weekend but seemingly came to an impasse on Sunday as the Clippers were asking for more than the Raptors were willing to give: three first-round picks and some of the Raptors' top young players. The Raptors turned away requests for all-NBA rookie Collin Murray-Boyles and breakout second-year wing Ja’Kobe Walter. Even as negotiations picked up on Monday, one league source suggested t

item.title: Once Los Angeles signalled that there wouldn’t be a …
item.description: Part of Leonard’s calculation, undoubtedly, was that the Raptors were willing to pay him, and the Clippers were not. Once Los Angeles signalled that there wouldn’t be a contract extension coming, Leonard — as one source described it — began working to “strongarm his way out of there.” He identified an opportunity with the Raptors and did what NBA superstars do: he put his thumb on the scale and made it happen.

item.title: When Kawhi Leonard left the Raptors, he was the …
item.description: Crazy Stats: When Kawhi Leonard left the Raptors, he was the winningest player in @NBA history (min. 300 games played, including playoffs). He is returning as the 11th-winningest player in league history.

item.title: Kings, Jonathan Mogbo agree to a two-way deal
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Sacramento Kings and Jonathan Mogbo have agreed to a two-way deal, league sources told @hoopshype. Mogbo was the 31st pick of the 2024 NBA Draft and spent the past two seasons with the Toronto Raptors. Agent Austin Walton of NEXT Sports and Kings officials agreed to the deal.

item.title: Mamu really wanted to be back in Toronto. Could’ve …
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: Mamu really wanted to be back in Toronto. Could’ve seen him taking a discount to stay if the $ was close, but Raptors were never gonna be able to compete with that, especially after taking on salary in the trade. Happy for Sandro. Lakers are a great fit & this is life-changing $

item.title: Raptors, Alijah Martin agree to two-year contract
item.description: Shams Charania: Guard Alijah Martin has agreed to a two-year, $4.8 million deal to return to the Toronto Raptors, sources tell ESPN. Martin was a second-round pick to Toronto in 2025 and enters a multiyear deal after the team and his agents, Todd Ramasar and Jaafar Choufani of Life Sports Agency finalized terms.

item.title: The matter of Kawhi Leonard’s trade kicker is still …
item.description: According to a team source, the matter of Kawhi Leonard’s trade kicker is still undetermined, with the Raptors considering several different structures to allow them to fill out their roster. A lot should be clearer by Monday at noon, when the moratorium is lifted.

item.title: Leonard isn’t in a position to accept his trade kicker …
item.description: However, because the Raptors used the salaries of Ingram and Dick to bring back more salary than they traded away in the form of Leonard, the Raptors are “hard-capped” at the first apron, which is set at a smidge beyond $209 million, this season. As of now, Leonard isn’t in a position to accept his trade kicker because Immanuel Quickley, RJ Barrett and Poeltl have $6.5 million worth of incentives that count toward the hard cap, even if they are not ultimately reached, per The Athletic’s John Hol

item.title: Raptors reward head coach Darko Rajakovic with multi-year contract extension
item.description: Michael Scotto: JUST IN: The Toronto Raptors have agreed to a multi-year contract extension with head coach Darko Rajakovic.

item.title: The Raptors say Collin Murray-Boyles has changed his …
item.description: Marc Stein: The Raptors say Collin Murray-Boyles has changed his jersey number from No. 12 to No. 30 … same as he wore at South Carolina.

item.title: The Raptors’ Summer League roster features sophomores …
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: The Raptors’ Summer League roster features sophomores Collin Murray-Boyles, Alijah Martin and Chucky Hepburn, as well as rookies Allen Graves and Jaden Bradley. Will be coached by Ivo Simovic

item.title: Kyle Anderson signs with Toronto
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent forward Kyle Anderson has agreed to a one-year, $3.9 million deal with the Toronto Raptors, sources tell ESPN. Anderson enters his 13th NBA season providing depth to the Raptors roster, and reunites with former San Antonio teammate Kawhi Leonard.

item.title: Kyle Lowry will retire as a Toronto Raptor
item.description: Kyle Lowry will be retiring as a Toronto Raptor. One of the most significant players in franchise history and the backbone of the most successful era the Raptors have ever had will follow through on a long-time pledge to retire with the team he led for nine seasons from 2012-13 to 2020-21

item.title: The team has planned an event and press conference for …
item.description: The team has planned an event and press conference for Tuesday, July 7th — Lowry wore No. 7 — billing it to invitees as a “monumental moment” in Raptors history, per sources. The event on Tuesday is expected to set the stage for additional recognition of the so-called GROAT (the Greatest Raptor of All-Time) as the 2026-27 season unfolds, per sources, with a likely jersey retirement planned at Scotiabank Arena during the regular season, where Lowry’s No. 7 would join Vince Carter’s No. 15 as the

item.title: Mavs looking to add Garrett Temple to Dusty May's coaching staff
item.description: Marc Stein: Also: @TheSteinLine has learned that new Mavericks coach Dusty May is also pursuing Garrett Temple for a spot on his first NBA coaching staff in the event that 16-year NBA veteran — who spent the past three seasons as a Toronto Raptor — elects to move into coaching.

item.title: For various reasons (injury/contractual, mostly), …
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: RJ Barrett, Zach Edey, Dwight Powell and Kelly Olynyk are here watching Canada-Puerto Rico. For various reasons (injury/contractual, mostly), they're not playing this summer but maintain spots in the country's player pool. Expected to be in the mix for the World Cup next summer.

item.title: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander on Raptors adding Kawhi Leonard: 'Adding a guy like that to your roster amplifies everything'
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: SGA on Kawhi returning to the Raptors: “For as long as I can remember, Toronto has loved the Raptors… I don’t think that’s ever going to change no matter who plays for them. But adding a guy like that to your roster amplifies everything… Toronto should be a good team next year”

item.title: Bummer for the show but will be cool to see Garrett …
item.description: Blake Murphy: Bummer for the show but will be cool to see Garrett Temple’s next chapter play out. He had considered still playing, coaching, front office, and law in the past. Obviously has a ton to teach, now as a coach instead of teammate.

item.title: Toronto Raptors and Jaden Bradley have agreed to a …
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Toronto Raptors and Jaden Bradley have agreed to a two-way deal, league sources told @hoopshype. Bradley was the 50th pick of the 2026 NBA Draft out of Arizona, where he was the Big 12 Player of the Year.

item.title: Overall, 84.4 percent of theoretical Poeltl trades in …
item.description: Poeltl has also most commonly been packaged with his teammate RJ Barrett in outgoing deals, as Toronto fans have used their combined 2026-27 salary of $49.1 million to go star-hunting in our trade machine. Overall, 84.4 percent of theoretical Poeltl trades in our trade machine have included Barrett.

item.title: Kawhi Leonard, Raptors to begin extension talks this week after hiring new agent
item.description: Kawhi Leonard and the Toronto Raptors are expected to start contract extension talks this week in Toronto after his trade becomes official -- and the two-time Finals MVP will do so with new representation. Leonard has hired Harrison Gaines of SLASH Sports as his new agent to oversee negotiations, sources told ESPN.

item.title: Gaines will meet with Raptors officials in Toronto …
item.description: Gaines will meet with Raptors officials in Toronto over the coming days to discuss the future of Leonard, who has one season and $50.3 million remaining on his contract. Gaines has worked closely with Leonard over the past year as his business attorney. The change is a major shift for Leonard, who previously had Mitch Frankel and his uncle and business adviser Dennis Robertson handle all of his affairs. Frankel represented Leonard on his negotiations with the LA Clippers, including a three-year

item.title: Leonard will now be eligible to sign up to a two-year, …
item.description: Three key reasons Leonard was open to this deal to Toronto, according to sources: Familiarity with a Toronto organization that has the majority of its front office structure in place, now led by executive vice president Bobby Webster. Leonard also likes the city of Toronto and believes the Raptors can contend in the Eastern Conference. Leonard envisions ending his career in Toronto, sources said. Leonard will now be eligible to sign up to a two-year, $123.7 million extension with his new team, a

item.title: Raptors 905 announce leadership changes
item.description: Zulfi Sheikh: Raptors 905 announce leadership changes. Scott Brown Jr. takes over as GM for Luke Winn and Noah Lewis will serve as the team's head coach, stepping in for Drew Jones. Raptors have swapped 905 leadership on two-year(ish) terms routinely to develop talent in-house.

item.title: Rogers Communications becomes 100 percent owner of Raptors' parent company
item.description: Rogers Communications Inc. signed a $4.35 billion agreement to become the 100 percent owner of Maple Leaf Sports and Entertainment, which owns the Toronto Maple Leafs and Toronto Raptors, buying the last 25 percent ownership stake from Kilmer Sports Inc., the company announced Monday. The deal is subject to league approvals, and Rogers said it expects it to close at the end of 2026.

item.title: Basketball was at the centre of several disagreements …
item.description: Basketball was at the centre of several disagreements within the old MLSE structure, however. Larry Tanenbaum wanted to bring a WNBA team to Toronto, but could not reach a consensus within the group. He eventually won a bid for an expansion team, which became the Toronto Tempo, who started play this season.

item.title: Kyle Lowry's official retirement with Raptors to come Tuesday?
item.description: Blake Murphy: The Raptors have announced a press conference for tomorrow morning, 7/7, with a “special guest” for a “milestone team announcement.” Kyle Lowry is teasing things on IG. Grange has reported. Etc. Fun morning ahead!

item.title: Veterans minimum signings such as DeMar DeRozan, …
item.description: Heat Central: Veterans minimum signings such as DeMar DeRozan, Bradley Beal, and Khris Middleton could be on hold for Miami until LeBron makes his decision per, @JakeLFischer “We’re looking at Toronto, potentially Miami. All these veterans who could go to Miami and join Giannis Antetokounmpo and Bam Adebayo, they I think will all be on hold with LeBron’s situation” (Via @BleacherReport)

item.title: Rogers Communications, the Canadian …
item.description: Rogers Communications, the Canadian communications/media giant that previously owned 75% of Maple Leafs Sport & Entertainment, has reached a deal to assume full control of the Raptors‘ parent company, per a press release from Rogers. Rogers held a 37.5% share of Maple Leaf Sports & Entertainment up until September 2024, when it bought out BCE (Bell)’s 37.5% stake in MLSE to increase its share of the company to 75%. The ownership agreement gave Rogers the right to buy out the remaining 25%, prima

item.title: Warriors expected to check in on DeMar DeRozan
item.description: Anthony Slater: You know I see the Miami Heat. I anticipate them being interested potentially in DeMar DeRozan. The Warriors have already been told it's a guy they will look into at some point in the coming days, especially if they do not get LeBron James. There's some heartwarming potential landing spots right. You know the Clippers play in his hometown the Raptors. He's so famous. Those are two teams that compete that could use him. And the reality is he should be coming at such a bargain that

item.title: Kawhi Leonard in the house, sitting front row for the …
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: Kawhi Leonard in the house, sitting front row for the Kyle Lowry retirement.

item.title: Kyle Lowry retires from NBA after 20 seasons
item.description: Kyle Lowry, who helped lead the Toronto Raptors to their lone championship in 2019, is retiring from the NBA. Generally agreed to be the greatest player in Raptors franchise history, the six-time All-Star guard announced his retirement with a video on Instagram on Tuesday, revealing his intentions to sign a one-day contract with the Raptors. The Raptors are expected to announce plans to retire his No. 7, which he wore for eight of his nine seasons in Toronto, during the 2026-27 season at a news

item.title: Kyle Lowry: It was only right to retire as a Raptor
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: Kyle Lowry: “I think this place has just been special for me since the day I got here. We had some rough times… but being around the city, country and organization, this is a special place… It was only right (to retire as a Raptor)”

item.title: Lowry signs his 1-day contract to retire as a Raptor
item.description: Esfandiar Baraheni: It’s official: Kyle Lowry signs his 1-day contract to retire as a Raptor.

item.title: Kyle Lowry about a potential statue: “It’d be an …
item.description: Esfandiar Baraheni: Asked Kyle Lowry about a potential statue: “It’d be an honour and very cool to be the only basketball guy next to all those hockey guys.” As for what it would be: “I’ll let the fans decide.”

item.title: Lowry on the Kawhi Leonard trade x2: “I appreciate him …
item.description: Esfandiar Baraheni: Kyle Lowry on the Kawhi Leonard trade x2: “I appreciate him coming here… just to be here for my moment. I think the moves that Bobby is making and what he’s doing, they know what they’re doing, they’re trying to win a championship… a championship is his only aspiration.”

item.title: Kyle Lowry signed a one-day contract with the Toronto …
item.description: Mark Medina: Kyle Lowry signed a one-day contract with the Toronto Raptors today and then announced he will retire following his 20-year NBA career

item.title: Toronto Tempo announced that NBA champion Kyle Lowry …
item.description: Grant Afseth: The Toronto Tempo announced that NBA champion Kyle Lowry and entrepreneur and philanthropist Ayahna Cornish-Lowry have joined the team's ownership group.

item.title: Pascal Siakam on Instagram congratulates former …
item.description: Tony East: Pascal Siakam on Instagram congratulates former Raptors teammate Kyle Lowry on his career and retirement:

item.title: DeMar DeRozan interested in going back to Raptors?
item.description: While DeRozan is interested in reuniting with the Raptors, NBA insider Jake Fischer revealed that the former NBA star might likely join teams that will fail to land James in this year's free agency. “He’s almost certainly going to be on a vet minimum somewhere else. Could it be Toronto? ... DeMar has interest in going back to the Raptors, but I think DeMar’s most likely landing spots are going to be teams that don’t get LeBron James," Fischer revealed on Bleacher Report. “I think the Miamis, Cle

item.title: Stephen Silas to join Raptors coaching staff
item.description: Michael Grange: Raptors notes from Las Vegas: look for longtime NBA assistant Stephen Silas to join Darko Rajakovic’s staff shortly. Silas has worked for Don Nelson, Rick Carlisle & his father, Paul Silas in 25yr career. Former head coach w Rockets, recently coached Team USA in WC Qualifying.

item.title: Former Raptors assistant Mike Batiste has taken a lead …
item.description: Michael Grange: Former Raptors assistant Mike Batiste has taken a lead assistant job with his old club Panathinaikos in the EuroLeague. James Wade won’t be back with Raptors.

item.title: Kawhi Leonard trade will not be complete until the NBA's investigation into the Clippers is over
item.description: Shams Charania: Developing: The Los Angeles Clippers-Toronto Raptors trade centered on Kawhi Leonard will not be complete until the NBA investigation into the Clippers is over. Statement from the Clippers to ESPN: For the past 10 months, our organization has fully cooperated with an NBA investigation, participating in dozens of interviews, providing tens of thousands of documents, and facilitating access to our staff. While the process has been challenging, we have remained committed to transpar

item.title: On June 30, we reached an agreement in principle to …
item.description: Shams Charania: Clippers statement: On June 30, we reached an agreement in principle to trade Kawhi Leonard to the Toronto Raptors. We have since been informed that the trade can only be finalized if the Raptors' ownership group assumes the risk of penalties related to Kawhi’s contract that could theoretically result from the ongoing investigation. The investigation is ongoing, and we expect the trade to be finalized following its conclusion.

item.title: We remain confident that, when the facts are evaluated …
item.description: Shams Charania: Clippers statement: At the heart of this investigation are Joe Sanberg and Aspiration. We did not funnel money to Kawhi Leonard through Aspiration. Like many sophisticated investors, financial institutions, and business partners, we were victims of a fraud initiated by Sanberg, who has been convicted and sentenced to 14 years in prison. We recognize the uncertainty this has created and the impact it has had on our team, our fans, the Raptors organization, their fans, and the play

item.title: We would assume the risk of any potential outcome of …
item.description: Marc Stein: Raptors statement: Regarding our planned trade with the LA Clippers for Kawhi Leonard, the NBA league office informed us that as a result of the ongoing investigation involving the Clippers, we would assume the risk of any potential outcome of the investigation impacting Kawhi. In light of this, we will wait until the league's investigation is complete. The Raptors remain eager to bring Kawhi back to Toronto and look forward to a swift resolution for our players, our organization and

item.title: Raptors hopeful Kawhi Leonard investigation is resolved by Tuesday?
item.description: Michael Grange: So, based on some preliminary convos: 1) NBA wants investigation complete before deal closes to avoid unintended consequences (ie small chance Kawhi contract gets voided) 2) Barring THAT the deal will close as agreed. 3) Hope is issue will be resolved by BOG meeting Tuesday:

item.title: Shams: Expectation is Kawhi Leonard trade will be done by end of the day
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Shams on Kawhi trade: "The expectation around all sides is that this deal will end up happening at the end of the day"

item.title: “We don’t have a specific timeline for the conclusion …
item.description: Marc Stein: NBA spokesperson to @TheSteinLine on the league’s investigation into Kawhi Leonard’s contract with the Clippers: “We don’t have a specific timeline for the conclusion of the investigation but expect the firm to finalize its work in the coming weeks."

item.title: If Kawhi Leonard faces a real penalty here we could …
item.description: NBABase: Windhorst: “The Clippers still want to do this trade badly they're getting a whole bunch of draft capital in this deal the Raptors in their statement yesterday said they were eager they want this trade to happen but if Kawhi Leonard faces a real penalty here we could see the trade terms reopened and I think that's what the Raptors are trying to protect themselves.”

item.title: Sources say, under the impression from the league …
item.description: The Clippers explored Leonard's trade market for weeks, sources say, before coming to terms on a deal with Toronto. And ... They did so, sources say, under the impression from the league office that the eventual outcome of the NBA's ongoing investigation into alleged cap impropriety involving Leonard and third-party business sponsor Aspiration would not have any impact on trading their star forward away.

item.title: Yet while the league’s handling of the situation isn’t …
item.description: The league ignored the public pressure that came from the trade being publicized, those sources say, and maintained its stance that trading for Leonard before the investigation was completed meant the Raptors were, as the team said in a statement Thursday, “assuming the risk of any potential outcome” of the investigation. Yet while the league’s handling of the situation isn’t seen as a sign that significant discipline is coming for the Clippers and/or Leonard, the potential for Leonard’s contrac

item.title: However small the risk might be that Leonard’s …
item.description: However small the risk might be that Leonard’s contract would be voided, that was reason enough for the Raptors to play it safe by cooperating with the league’s preference to close the investigation before finalizing the trade. The NBA has only voided the contract of one player, Joe Smith, and that was after the Minnesota Timberwolves signed him to three consecutive below-market deals with the promise to sign him to a longer, richer deal in the future. In that case, the league voided Smith’s con

item.title: Regardless, the sense is that both teams are not …
item.description: Regardless, the sense is that both teams are not looking to alter the terms of the deal. Leonard was already at the Raptors practice facility in Toronto earlier in the week, and was in attendance for Kyle Lowry’s retirement announcement on Tuesday. Ingram and Dick were in Las Vegas Thursday night watching the Clippers’ summer league team.

item.title: There is a general bewilderment around the league …
item.description: There is a general bewilderment around the league regarding how long this investigation has lasted, and it’s not unreasonable to think the players’ association would become involved if it continued long enough to jeopardize Leonard’s ability to plan his future — i.e., sign an extension, as his current contract is set to expire after this coming season. The league never suspended Leonard or placed him on leave during the investigation, so the possibility that the Clippers might look to trade him

item.title: The NBA informed the Raptors before they agreed to the …
item.description: The NBA informed the Raptors before they agreed to the trade that the investigation could cause issues with any deal involving Leonard, according to sources briefed on the matter who spoke on the condition of anonymity because the league’s probe is ongoing. Yet when the terms of the deal between the Clippers and Raptors were widely reported on June 30, it forced the league — and commissioner Adam Silver — to decide how to handle this unprecedented situation.

item.title: The Toronto Raptors have signed center Nate Bittle to …
item.description: Keith Smith: The Toronto Raptors have signed center Nate Bittle to an Exhibit 10 contract, a league source tells @spotrac.

item.title: The expectation is that Brandon Ingram will remain …
item.description: AP Hoops: The expectation is that Brandon Ingram will remain with the LA Clippers once the Kawhi Leonard trade is finalized, per @JakeLFischer . Ingram being rerouted to a third team is not considered likely at this juncture.

item.title: New NBPA executive director David Kelly says “it …
item.description: Jake Fischer: New NBPA executive director David Kelly says “it should not take this long,” in regards to the NBA’s investigation into Kawhi Leonard, Los Angeles and Aspiration.

item.title: David Kelly also said he does not think “there’s a …
item.description: Jake Fischer: David Kelly also said he does not think “there’s a there there,” with Kawhi Leonard, stating the union agrees with the Clippers that they expect the investigation won’t find any impropriety from Leonard’s endorsement deal with Aspiration functioning as salary cap circumvention.

item.title: Barnes on the Raptors potential addition of Kawhi …
item.description: Fullcourtpass: Scottie Barnes on the Raptors potential addition of Kawhi Leonard "Seems very dangerous... We’re trying to take candy from a baby essentially."

item.title: “My first thoughts are that I’ve had a lot of battles …
item.description: Like almost everyone else, though, Nurse was floored by Philly acquiring Jaylen Brown from the Boston Celtics. Nurse has spent his summer bracing for a roster with a suddenly higher floor and ceiling after the 76ers had 45 wins and the East’s No. 7 seed last season. More importantly, his preparation for coaching Brown may mirror his prep for Kawhi Leonard, who led the Nurse-coached Toronto Raptors to the 2019 NBA title. “My first thoughts are that I’ve had a lot of battles against him,” Nurse to

item.title: Chris Haynes on the Kawhi trade:: “The Raptors, they …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Chris Haynes on the Kawhi trade:: “The Raptors, they were very comfortable in making this deal but they did not know that the league was going to put a halt on the trade. Both sides still feel like this thing will be resolved, a trade will occur. Brandon Ingram, Gradey Dick, they're all on the Clippers bench as if they're official Clipper members"

item.title: Norman Powell: My lowest point was my third year in the league in Toronto
item.description: Norman Powell: My lowest point was my third year in the league in Toronto. I just came off of a great playoff run in my second year in the league helping them close out a win series and I signed my contract extension and I worked extremely hard. Looking back I kind of overworked myself and not being smart about how I took care of my body in the summer and I crashed and burned during the season. I had a really bad season and I signed my contract before and just everybody was talking about like I

item.title: Kawhi Leonard greets Raptors brass during Summer League game
item.description: Doug Smith: Kawhi Leonard sauntering up to mid-court to visit with Larry and Judy Tanenbaum and Bobby Webster might be of note to Raptors fans who absolutely won’t read anything into it

item.title: Kawhi Leonard investigation has grown in scope, including previously unreported endorsement deal with a second company
item.description: While the LA Clippers, Leonard and now the Raptors all wait for a conclusion, the investigation has grown in scope since it began, according to multiple sources who spoke to The Athletic on the condition of anonymity in order to speak freely. Not only has Wachtell Lipton, the league’s go-to law firm hired to conduct the inquiry, inspected if the Clippers circumvented the NBA salary cap by facilitating a sponsorship deal for Leonard with Aspiration, it has also looked into whether the Clippers im

item.title: It does show how the probe is already creating …
item.description: While a team source denied that the delay is an implication that Toronto fears or expects some penalty, it does show how the probe is already creating consequences even as it continues to examine possible cap circumvention by the Clippers.

item.title: Former Aspiration CEO Andrei Cherny signed off on the …
item.description: Former Aspiration CEO Andrei Cherny signed off on the contract with Leonard, though Sanberg brought the Clippers star in, multiple sources said. The sponsorship deal, sources said, was worth significantly more than the endorsement deals Aspiration reached with the musician Drake, who invested $4 million and received carbon credits, and actor Robert Downey Jr., who received less than $2 million in equity. Sanberg also gave Leonard $20 million in equity in the company, The Athletic reported in Sep

item.title: The deal with the Clippers and a relationship with …
item.description: The deal with the Clippers and a relationship with Ballmer was driven by Sanberg, sources said. Ballmer had “a great relationship with Joe,” one former Aspiration executive said, beyond just sharing a dais together during a 2021 news conference that announced Aspiration as a jersey patch partner for the Clippers.

item.title: The Chicago Bulls will add Rex Kalamian, Jonah Herscu, …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Chicago Bulls will add Rex Kalamian, Jonah Herscu, and Blake Ahearn as assistant coaches. Kalamian was an assistant coach for the Bucks, Pistons, Kings, Clippers, Raptors, Thunder, Timberwolves, and Nuggets. Herscu was Portland’s G League head coach and assistant coach for the Blazers and Kings. Ahearn was a Grizzlies assistant coach and San Antonio’s G League head coach.

item.title: Adam Silver hopes Kawhi Leonard trade wraps up during the summer
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Adam Silver on the Kawhi trade: "I'm hopeful that it will wrap up this summer. The league did not pause the trade. The parties to the trade made a decision not to go forward given that the investigation remained open. The investigation needs to run its course"

item.title: "The issues were well known to the teams. I don't …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Adam Silver: "The issues were well known to the teams. I don't think any of this information should've been a surprise to anyone involved"

item.title: It is realistic to think that this can be wrapped up. …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Adam Silver: "I don't know specifically what will be in the report. It is realistic to think that this can be wrapped up. It's going on longer than I would have hoped"

item.title: The league did not pause the trade."
item.description: Ben Golliver: NBA Commissioner Adam Silver on the Kawhi Leonard investigation: "My timeline remains this summer. I’m not conducting the investigation. ... The league did not pause the trade."

item.title: DeMar DeRozan interested in Raptors reunion
item.description: According to a source close to the beloved former Toronto all-star, DeRozan would welcome a Raptors return “if everything works out,” while other sources — also granted anonymity in order to speak freely on the subject — say the Raptors front office has been thinking about the possibility for some time.

item.title: Raptors unworried about Kawhi Leonard situation?
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: For what it’s worth, Raptors continue to talk/act like a team that’s unworried. Sense is the only thing that would jeopardize the trade is the unlikely scenario where Kawhi’s contract is voided. Any other possible penalty, including suspension, is something they could work around

item.title: "The absolute worst is Kawhi Leonard's contract being …
item.description: Depending on the evidence uncovered, the Los Angeles Clippers star could reportedly face serious disciplinary action, ranging from a lengthy suspension to the possible voiding of his contract. According to ESPN's Shams Charania, even a full-season suspension cannot be ruled out at this stage. "The absolute worst is Kawhi Leonard's contract being voided, or him being suspended for a significant period of time. Whether it's 40/20 games or entire season.. it all comes down to the evidence and what'

item.title: Steve Ballmer met with lawyers last week in what was likely one of the final steps of Kawhi Leonard investigation
item.description: Ballmer has previously sat for an interview with the Wachtell lawyers, and the Clippers owner met with them again last week in New York City in what sources with knowledge of the matter said was probably one of the final steps of the investigation.

item.title: A source with direct knowledge of the situation said …
item.description: Silver declined to provide details on how or when teams such as the Raptors were informed about the associated risks of acquiring him. A source with direct knowledge of the situation said the NBA did not communicate with the Clippers in advance about the matter because they never requested guidance from the league office.

item.title: Raptors paused Kawhi Leonard trade when asked to assume risk of any penalties from Clippers contract investigation
item.description: The tipping point to put on hold the trade of Kawhi Leonard to the Raptors came when it was time for Toronto to officially sign off on the conditions of the deal.
