According to multiple NBA sources, when the Raptors had to commit in writing to assume any risk of any penalties Leonard might incur from an investigation into his Los Angeles Clippers contract, they decided to pause on the transaction.

item.title: The Raptors knowing they might be on the hook for …
item.description: The sources said here Tuesday that the Raptors knowing they might be on the hook for future penalties was one thing; having to sign off on formal conditions was enough to make Toronto decide to “just wait” until the probe is completed.

item.title: The Raptors don’t seem overly concerned at the pace of …
item.description: The Raptors don’t seem overly concerned at the pace of the process and sources insist they think the deal as proposed will be consummated. And, in truth, it’s not like the Raptors have a lot of reason to rush it. The way the summer player market has unfolded, the team’s salary cap situation and the kind of free agents still available, there’s no other major deals to make.

item.title: I am hearing that they want to get this done, that …
item.description: Marc J. Spears: "I think the assumption is that the Clippers are fearful of what is to come, but I am hearing that they want to get this done, that they feel like Steve Ballmer was a victim, that he is going to be innocent. We will see. But they are eager to get this done, and there is deeper than the Clippers. There are so many teams, players. "

item.title: “I talked to a team exec a few months ago. I asked him …
item.description: Amin Elhassan: “I talked to a team exec a few months ago. I asked him ‘what do you think the other teams are rooting for as an outcome [for the Clippers investigation]?’ and he says to me ‘I hope they get dinged because if not my job becomes money launderer.’”

item.title: Collin Murray-Boyles on Raptors: 'If we stay healthy, I think we're a Top 3 team in the East for sure'
item.description: Collin Murray-Boyles: "If we stay healthy, I think we're a top-three team in the East for sure. Obviously, the East got way deeper this year, but I think that we're up there with the best of the best. If we can stay consistent, I think we had a very young team last year. A lot of our pieces that were playing in the playoffs, that was their first time. So, just everybody taking what we learned from the playoffs, how we approach each game, the things that we didn't do as well that we needed to do,

item.title: Conclusion to Kawhi Leonard investigation still weeks away?
item.description: Pat McAffee: Shams Charania: "When the Clippers and Raptors went to do a trade call the league told them they couldn't go thru with this.. The Kawhi Leonard deal is paused and from my understanding we're still weeks out from a resolution"

item.title: Pistons pursuing DeMar DeRozan?
item.description: The Pistons are still evaluating the prospect of pursuing DeMar DeRozan in free agency, league sources say. It is not yet certain if Detroit will indeed join Toronto as a confirmed bidder for the six-time former All-Star guard, but The Stein Line did learn this week that the Pistons have been assessing the concept.

item.title: The Atlanta Hawks, who recently declined a team option …
item.description: The Milwaukee Bucks have emerged as a team potentially interested in executing a sign-and-trade for Watson, according to league sources who were granted anonymity so that they could describe ongoing deliberations. You can add them to a list of potential suitors that includes the LA Clippers, who are now in a holding pattern after their Kawhi Leonard trade with the Toronto Raptors was paused due to the NBA’s ongoing Aspiration investigation. The Atlanta Hawks, who recently declined a team option

item.title: “We kind of got pushed out [last season],” Van Vleet …
item.description: VanVleet said he is using his 2019 NBA championship with the Raptors and the New York Knicks’ 2026 NBA championship as motivation for his Rockets next season. “We kind of got pushed out [last season],” Van Vleet said. “We went from a fun AAU team to adding Kevin to a little favorite. The only bad thing about being a favorite is you get all of the flack — all of the criticism first. So, my experience is winning a championship in Toronto when nobody cared. Nobody paid us any attention. And they di

item.title: It certainly seems at least possible that we have a …
item.description: Seemingly, the only pathways to an expeditious resolution are if the investigation produces no evidence of wrongdoing — can you imagine if, after all this time, Wachtell comes back and says, “We got nothing?” — or if Steve Ballmer and the Clippers basically “plea bargain” to certain penalties and don’t go through the remaining steps of the process. So how does this get resolved? And can the Clippers and Raptors get their Leonard trade to the finish line before the season starts? It certainly see
